assertion_results.text: Description includes a Summary section with a 1-3 sentence overview mentioning OAuth2 or Google login
assertion_results.evidence: Summary section present: 'Adds Google OAuth2 login as an alternative authentication method, allowing users to sign in with their Google accounts'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Motivation section explaining WHY (user feedback, 60% requested social login)
assertion_results.evidence: Motivation section cites '60% of beta users requested social login in our Q1 feedback survey' and '80% of our users already have Google accounts'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Changes section that groups changes by concept (auth flow, data model, configuration) rather than listing files one by one
assertion_results.evidence: Changes grouped as: 'OAuth2 authentication flow', 'Route integration', 'Session management', 'User model', 'Database migration', 'Configuration', 'Tests'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Testing Instructions section with numbered, copy-pasteable steps for verifying the OAuth flow
assertion_results.evidence: 13 numbered steps with exact commands including 'npm test -- --grep oauth2', navigation URLs, and database verification queries

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Rollback Plan section that addresses the database migration (ALTER TABLE) and how to reverse it
assertion_results.evidence: Rollback plan includes 'Run the down migration: npm run migrate:down 20260305_add_oauth_fields' and notes data impact for OAuth users

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Reviewer Notes section that flags security-sensitive areas (token handling, OAuth callback, session management)
assertion_results.evidence: Reviewer Notes flags token validation (oauth2.ts:34-58), session token storage (session.ts:22-35), and profile mapping fallbacks

assertion_results.text: Testing instructions include at least one edge case or error scenario (e.g., user denies consent, invalid token, duplicate Google account)
assertion_results.evidence: Edge cases listed under 'Edge cases to verify' section (deny consent, duplicate account) but they are not fully copy-pasteable — step 12 says 'Deny consent on the Google screen' without specifying how to trigger this in a test environment

assertion_results.text: Rollback plan mentions the database migration specifically and whether it is reversible
assertion_results.evidence: States 'The migration is reversible — it only ADDs columns, and the down migration DROPs them' with data impact note
